brainstormIdeas.line4: For each idea include a brief description and potential impact
brainstormIdeas.line5: Categorize ideas by feasibility (quick win, medium effort, moonshot)
brainstormIdeas.line6: Include pros and cons for the top 3 ideas
brainstormIdeas.line7: Consider the target audience and market trends
brainstormIdeas.line8: Add competitive analysis for the most promising ideas
brainstormIdeas.line9: Suggest potential MVP features for the top idea
brainstormIdeas.line10: Include rough effort estimates where possible

brainstormProduct.line3: Start by identifying the top 5 user pain points this product should solve
brainstormProduct.line4: For each idea: name it, describe it in 1-2 sentences, and rate its impact (high/medium/low)
brainstormProduct.line5: Categorize ideas: must-have for MVP, nice-to-have, future roadmap
brainstormProduct.line6: Include at least 2 innovative/unconventional ideas that competitors don't offer
brainstormProduct.line7: For the top 3 ideas, suggest how to monetize them
brainstormProduct.line8: Assess competitive landscape: who already does something similar?
brainstormProduct.line9: Suggest a minimal MVP scope that could launch in 4-6 weeks
brainstormProduct.line10: Rank all ideas by effort vs. impact and highlight the quick wins

brainstormMarketing.line4: For each campaign: name, channel, target audience, core message, and expected outcome
brainstormMarketing.line5: Include a mix of channels: organic, paid, partnerships, and community
brainstormMarketing.line6: Suggest 3 viral/guerrilla marketing ideas that could punch above the budget
brainstormMarketing.line7: Define KPIs for each campaign: what metric defines success?
brainstormMarketing.line8: Create a 30-60-90 day launch plan with the highest-priority campaigns first
brainstormMarketing.line9: Include messaging frameworks: headline, subheadline, and key selling points
brainstormMarketing.line10: Identify potential partnerships or influencers that could amplify reach

brainstormContent.line4: For each idea: title, format (article/video/thread/etc.), and target audience segment
brainstormContent.line5: Include a mix of: educational, entertaining, inspirational, and promotional content
brainstormContent.line6: Suggest 5 evergreen topics that will remain relevant for years
brainstormContent.line7: Suggest 5 trending/timely topics to capitalize on right now
brainstormContent.line8: For each piece, suggest a hook or headline that would stop someone scrolling
brainstormContent.line9: Organize into a content calendar: which to publish first and at what frequency
brainstormContent.line10: Suggest repurposing strategies: how to turn 1 piece into 5 across platforms

brainstormDesign.line3: Map out the 3 most critical user flows end to end
brainstormDesign.line4: Suggest a color palette and typography system that matches the brand personality
brainstormDesign.line5: Propose layout ideas for the key screens (describe or wireframe in text)
brainstormDesign.line6: Suggest micro-interactions and animations that improve the user experience
brainstormDesign.line7: Identify accessibility requirements and how to meet WCAG 2.1 AA
brainstormDesign.line8: Suggest 3 design patterns from successful apps in a similar space
brainstormDesign.line9: Propose a responsive strategy: how should the design adapt from mobile to desktop?
brainstormDesign.line10: List 5 usability pitfalls to avoid for this type of product

brainstormStrategy.line4: Conduct a SWOT analysis: strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, threats
brainstormStrategy.line5: Identify the top 3 growth levers available right now
brainstormStrategy.line6: Propose 3 strategic initiatives with clear objectives and key results (OKRs)
brainstormStrategy.line7: Map the competitive landscape: where are the gaps we can exploit?
brainstormStrategy.line8: Identify potential strategic partnerships or acquisition targets
brainstormStrategy.line9: Assess the top 3 risks and propose mitigation strategies for each
brainstormStrategy.line10: Create a prioritized roadmap: what to do in month 1, quarter 1, and quarter 2

brainstormNaming.line4: Include a mix: real words, coined words, compound words, and acronyms
brainstormNaming.line5: Check that each name is easy to spell, pronounce, and remember
brainstormNaming.line6: Verify .com domain availability likelihood (avoid common dictionary words)
brainstormNaming.line7: For each name, add a one-line tagline or slogan that pairs well
brainstormNaming.line8: Flag any names that might have negative connotations in other languages
brainstormNaming.line9: Group names by style: playful, professional, abstract, descriptive
brainstormNaming.line10: Highlight your top 3 picks and explain why they stand out

brainstormEvent.line4: Suggest a theme that ties the whole event together
brainstormEvent.line5: Create a timeline: pre-event prep, day-of schedule, and follow-up
brainstormEvent.line6: List must-have logistics: venue, catering, A/V, signage, parking
brainstormEvent.line7: Propose 3-5 engaging activities or interactive elements
brainstormEvent.line8: Include a plan for promotion and attendee engagement before the event
brainstormEvent.line9: Add contingency plans for common issues (weather, no-shows, tech failures)
brainstormEvent.line10: Suggest metrics to measure event success and gather attendee feedback

brainstormProblemSolving.line3: Use first-principles thinking — break the problem down to its root causes
brainstormProblemSolving.line4: Consider solutions from adjacent industries or domains
brainstormProblemSolving.line5: For each solution, estimate: effort (low/med/high), impact (low/med/high), timeline
brainstormProblemSolving.line6: Include at least 2 unconventional or contrarian approaches
brainstormProblemSolving.line7: Identify what data or evidence would validate each solution
brainstormProblemSolving.line8: List the assumptions behind each approach and how to test them
brainstormProblemSolving.line9: Suggest a phased approach: quick wins first, then structural changes
brainstormProblemSolving.line10: Recommend which solution to prototype first and why

brainstormStartup.line1: Brainstorm startup ideas in the {{domain}} space
brainstormStartup.line3: For each idea: one-line pitch, target customer, and revenue model
brainstormStartup.line4: Focus on real pain points — what are people currently hacking together or overpaying for?
brainstormStartup.line5: Include a mix: SaaS, marketplace, API/tool, content, and service-based models
brainstormStartup.line6: Estimate market size (TAM) for the top ideas
brainstormStartup.line7: Identify existing competitors and what a new entrant could do differently
brainstormStartup.line8: Suggest an MVP scope that could be built in 2-4 weeks
brainstormStartup.line9: Flag which ideas have network effects or natural moats
brainstormStartup.line10: Rank ideas by: ease of starting solo, revenue potential, and personal passion fit

brainstormSideProject.line1: Suggest side project ideas using my skills in {{skill}}
brainstormSideProject.line4: Include projects that could become portfolio pieces or resume highlights
brainstormSideProject.line5: Mix practical (solve a real problem) and fun (learn something new) projects
brainstormSideProject.line6: For each project: brief description, tech stack suggestion, and difficulty level
brainstormSideProject.line7: Include at least 2 projects that could potentially generate passive income
brainstormSideProject.line8: Suggest projects that are impressive to show in job interviews
brainstormSideProject.line9: Add a 'stretch goal' version for each project if I want to go deeper
brainstormSideProject.line10: Recommend which project to start with based on learning value and fun factor
